atd.actions.title: Fusion Actions
atd.actions.hint: Write-back actions performed inside Oracle Fusion (e.g. create AP invoice from an approved Petty Cash reimbursement). The runner performs each via the shared browser session; idempotent — never creates a duplicate.
atd.discovery.title: OTBI Discovery
atd.schedgen.title: Generate Schedule OTBI Data
atd.schedgen.hint: Point at a catalog folder and generate the incremental schedule variants (Full _F, Hourly _UH, 10-min _U10M) for every base analysis in it. The date filter auto-detects the “Last Updated Date” column; existing variants are skipped.
atd.schedgen.queued: Queued — the runner will generate the variants.
atd.discovery.hint: Scrape an OTBI subject area's folders + columns so they can be picked when building an analysis. Queue a subject area here, then the runner (python runner.py --discover) scrapes it.
atd.discovery.saPlaceholder: Subject area name, e.g. Procurement - Purchasing Real Time
atd.discovery.needSa: Enter a subject area name first.
atd.discovery.queued: Queued — the runner scrapes it on the next discover cycle (python runner.py --discover).
atd.discovery.noRequests: No subject areas discovered yet. Enter one above and click Discover.
atd.rs.title: Runner Settings
atd.rs.intro: Operational settings the runner reads at startup (DB overrides env.ps1). Secrets (passwords, bot token) are NOT here — they stay in env.ps1 / OCI Vault.
atd.rs.saved: Settings saved — applied on the next run
atd.dash.title: Analytics Loader
atd.dash.refreshActualsHint: Rebuild the GL classification snapshot the actuals reporting views read (run after a load).
atd.dash.noWorkers: No workers have reported in yet.
atd.workers.refresh.hint: Force this worker to re-login to Fusion (approve the Authenticator number when it arrives)
atd.workers.refresh.confirm: Ask {vm} to re-login to Fusion now? You'll need to approve the Microsoft Authenticator number.
atd.workers.refresh.asked: Re-login requested for {vm} — approve the Authenticator number when it arrives.
atd.legend.title: Status legend
atd.legend.WARNING: The run completed but flagged a warning (e.g. a schema drift) — review the message.
atd.legend.READY: Queued — waiting for a free worker to pick it up.
atd.legend.CLAIMED: A worker has claimed the job and is running it now.
atd.legend.DONE: Last queue run finished without error.
atd.legend.SUCCESS: Data was extracted from OTBI and loaded into the target table.
atd.legend.HELD: Target table was prepared but no data was loaded — the job is on hold awaiting schema review. Open the job and approve the columns to release it.
atd.legend.REQUEUED: The Fusion session expired mid-run; the job was handed back to the queue to retry automatically (no data lost).
atd.legend.FAILED: The run hit an error — open Run Logs for the message.
atd.jobs.title: Jobs
atd.action.run.hint: Run now — moves this job to the front of the queue; it runs on the next worker cycle (≤15 min)
atd.action.delete.hint: Delete this job (run history is kept)
atd.action.remap.hint: Clear the stored column map — the next run re-derives it from the live analysis (table data is kept)
atd.action.rebuild.hint: Drop and recreate the table from the live analysis on the next run — accepts an incompatible column change; currently loaded rows are lost
atd.field.targetTable.ph: PROD.ATD_<name> — created on first run if blank
atd.field.columnMap.hint: Leave blank — the runner builds it from the live CSV on the first run.
atd.field.frequency.hint: How often this job is queued. Blank uses the default; e.g. 60 = hourly, 1440 = daily.
atd.cat.label: Categories
atd.cat.inUse: In use — deactivate instead of deleting
atd.review.hold.hint: Prepare the table, but don't load any data until you approve the structure.
atd.review.badge.hint: Held — awaiting schema review before loading
atd.review.approve.hint: Release this job — it loads on its next run
atd.review.notice.hint: The staging table is prepared. Review the structure below (edit it if needed), then approve to start loading.
atd.review.confirmApprove: Approve this table structure and start loading on the next run?
atd.review.approved: Schema approved — the job will load on its next run
atd.review.createdHold: Job {name} created — held for schema review (won't load until approved)
atd.review.createdExisting: Job {name} created — target table already exists, so it's held for schema review
atd.cat.deleteFailed: Could not delete — it may be in use; deactivate it instead
atd.jobs.minimalHint: Just the analysis path is needed. The table, columns and mapping are prepared automatically on the first run.
atd.jobs.createdPrepare: Job "{name}" created — table & columns are prepared on first run.
atd.analysis.title: Add New OTBI Analysis
atd.analysis.name: Analysis Name
atd.analysis.col.heading: Heading (optional)
atd.analysis.hint: Folder + Column match the OTBI subject-area pane labels; Heading renames the output column (optional).
atd.analysis.needColumn: Add at least one column (folder + column).
atd.analysis.queued: Queued — the runner builds it in OTBI on the next build cycle (python runner.py --build).
atd.analysis.queueHint: Optional prompted filters as JSON. The runner builds the analysis, then registers + loads it as a job.
atd.analysis.discovered: Or pick a discovered subject area
atd.analysis.pickHint: Tick a column to add it below; untick to remove. Headings stay editable.
atd.analysis.discoverQueued: Discovery queued — it scrapes automatically within ~a minute; reopen to pick columns.
atd.analysis.notDiscovered: Not discovered yet — click "Discover columns" to scrape this subject area.
atd.analysis.status.queued: Discovery queued — scrapes automatically within ~a minute; reopen shortly.
atd.analysis.status.failed: Discovery failed — see the runner log; try again.
atd.analysis.suggest: Describe the data you need (AI suggests columns)
atd.analysis.suggestPlaceholder: e.g. supplier number, name, site, city, country and bank account for active suppliers
atd.analysis.suggestHint: AI picks matching columns from this subject area's discovered catalog and ticks them below — review and edit before Build.
atd.analysis.suggestNeed: Describe the data you need first.
atd.analysis.suggestNone: No matching columns suggested — try rephrasing, or pick manually.
atd.jobs.confirmDelete: Delete this job? Run history is kept.
atd.jobs.enqueued: Queued for the next worker run
atd.jobs.confirmRun: Run this job now? It moves to the front of the queue and runs on the next worker cycle.
atd.jobs.runQueued: Moved to front — runs next worker cycle
atd.jobs.confirmRemap:
Re-map this job's columns?

The stored column map is cleared and re-derived from the live analysis on the next run. The table and its loaded rows are kept.
atd.jobs.confirmRebuild:
Rebuild this job's table?

This DROPS the stage table (and final table, if any) and recreates it from the live analysis on the next run — all currently loaded rows are LOST.

Use this to accept an incompatible column change (a NUMBER/DATE column the analysis now sends as text).
atd.jobs.remapped: Column map cleared — re-derived on the next run
atd.jobs.rebuilt: Table dropped — rebuilt from the analysis on the next run
atd.detail.title: Job
atd.schema.title: Table Schema
atd.schema.hint: Edit column names, data types and the table name. Nothing changes until you click Apply — the staging table is recreated from this definition and reloaded on the next run (it holds load-only data, so no rows are lost permanently).
atd.schema.noTable: No table yet — run the job once to create it, then review here.
atd.schema.removeHint: Remove this column (takes effect when you click Apply; Reload restores it)
atd.schema.col.name: Column name
atd.schema.confirmApply:
Apply this schema?

The staging table is dropped and recreated from these column names / data types (and table name), then reloaded on the next run. Current rows are cleared and reloaded.
atd.schema.applied: Schema applied — table recreated; it reloads on the next run
atd.queue.title: Queue & Operations
atd.queue.reap.hint: Return jobs stuck as CLAIMED longer than the set minutes (a worker grabbed one but crashed/restarted before finishing) back to the queue so another worker runs them.
atd.queue.lease.hint: Only recover jobs that have been claimed longer than this many minutes.
atd.queue.hint: Enqueue marks jobs READY; a worker host running the runner picks them up.
atd.runs.title: Run Logs
atd.runs.detail.title: Run
atd.envs.title: Environments
atd.envs.col.name: Name
atd.targets.title: Targets
atd.targets.col.name: Name
atd.review.notice.pending.hint: The schema is captured on the first successful run — source headers and column types appear here once the job extracts the source once. (If a run looks stuck, refresh the worker so the extract can complete.)
atd.set.title: Job Sets
atd.set.intro: Group jobs under one shared schedule — a run interval, an active window and optional day/time mask — then enable/disable, pause or run the whole batch together. Jobs not in a set keep their own schedule.
atd.set.empty: No job sets yet. Click New Set to create one.
atd.set.detail.title: Job Set
atd.set.run.hint: Queue every enabled member now at top priority — they run on the next worker cycle (bypasses the window).
atd.set.days.hint: Leave all unselected to run every day. Times/dates are local (Asia/Dubai).
atd.set.notify.hint: Notify the admins when any member of this set has a failed run (needs the failure-notify sweep enabled in Runner Settings).
atd.set.window.hint: Members only run while now is inside the window (Start/End dates + optional daily time + selected days). Leave a field blank for no bound.
atd.set.members.hint: A job can belong to one set. Toggle “In set” to hold a member without removing it.
atd.set.noMembers: No jobs in this set yet — add one from the picker above.
atd.set.toggleHint: Click to enable / disable this member within the set
atd.set.confirmRun: Run every enabled member of this set now?
atd.set.confirmDelete: Delete the set {name}? Its jobs are kept (only the grouping is removed).
atd.set.pausedNotice.hint: Members are not scheduled while the set is paused. Existing jobs still run individually only if removed from the set.
